label: Estimated War Deaths (2011–2024)
contestNote: Syrian Observatory for Human Rights (SOHR) documented ~580,000 deaths; Syrian Network for Human Rights counted ~232,000 civilian deaths alone; Russian and Assad-regime sources minimized figures; true total may exceed 600,000 including those missing.

label: Total Displaced Persons
deltaNote: Early voluntary returns since December 2024, mostly from Turkey and Lebanon

label: Refugees Registered Abroad
deltaNote: Net figure — some returning, some still fleeing conflict zones

label: Transitional Govt Territory Control
source: ACLED / ISW Syria Control Map April 2025
contestNote: HTS/transitional government controls Damascus, Aleppo, Homs, Hama, Idlib, Latakia, Daraa, and much of southern Syria. Estimates vary due to contested areas and tribal zones.

label: SDF (AANES) Territory Control
source: ACLED / ISW Syria Control Map April 2025
contestNote: SDF/AANES controls northeast Syria (Jazira, Euphrates basin including Raqqa, Deir ez-Zor NE, Qamishli). Turkey and transitional govt dispute legitimacy of SDF self-governance.

label: Estimated Reconstruction Cost
source: World Bank / UNDP Syria Needs Assessment 2024
contestNote: World Bank 2017 estimate was $226B; subsequent destruction raised estimates. Some analysts cite $400B+; the actual figure depends heavily on security environment and population return.

label: GDP vs Pre-War Level
source: World Bank / IMF Syria Economic Monitor 2024
contestNote: Comprehensive economic data from Syria is unreliable. Syrian pound devaluation, black market economy, and sanctions make comparisons difficult. Some analysts place current GDP as low as 15% of pre-war levels.
